notes: Pickleheads lists '5 Most Popular Pickleball Courts in Milpitas, CA' totaling 16 courts (Hall Memorial Park 6, Gill Park 2, Milpitas Sports Center 6, Indoor Private Pickleball court 1, Silicon Valley Table Tennis and Pickleball 1). Cross-checking against the city's own official Pickle Ball Pilot Program page (milpitas.gov/832/Pickle-Ball-Pilot-Program — the primary source per this research's brief) and independent sources turned up a likely duplicate: 'Indoor Private Pickleball court' and 'Silicon Valley Table Tennis and Pickleball' share the same street address (1827 Houret Ct), an identical facility description (1 indoor acrylic court, dedicated, permanent net/lines, private, reservable), and a Yelp review of the former exactly matches the latter's own pricing and hidden-entrance/keypad access details — strong evidence Pickleheads split one real single-court venue into two listings. Merged them into one venue record here (id: silicon-valley-table-tennis-and-pickleball) rather than padding the count with a probable duplicate — see that venue's own notes for the full evidence trail. Also added one venue Pickleheads does not list at all: The Picklr - Milpitas, a new 16-court indoor club at McCarthy Ranch that, per its own website, opened the day before this research (July 10, 2026) — found via general web search and a Milpitas Beat news feature, not Pickleheads. Net result: 5 venues in this file (Hall Memorial Park, Peter Gill Memorial Park, Milpitas Sports Center, Silicon Valley Table Tennis and Pickleball, The Picklr - Milpitas) totaling 31 courts (15 Pickleheads-corroborated + 16 from The Picklr) — a different set of 5 than Pickleheads' own 5, though the same headline location count. The two city-run outdoor sites (Hall Memorial Park, Gill Memorial Park) are not dedicated pickleball construction — they're a still-technically-temporary 'one-year pilot program' (started August 2022) that striped pickleball lines onto existing tennis and handball courts. The striping is still in place and courts are actively in use as of 2026-dated sources, but whether they become permanent is a genuinely open and, at Hall Memorial Park specifically, mildly contested question (city council left its status undecided as of the most recent update found, May/June 2025) — flagged in detail on that venue's own record. Milpitas has no existing cities/milpitas.html page or courts-data.json/venues.json entries yet, so every venue here is matchStatus 'new' with no baseline to conflict against; the one real conflict found (The Picklr's indoor vs. outdoor description) is a source-vs-source disagreement, not a baseline-vs-Pickleheads one, so it's documented in that venue's notes rather than forced into the conflicts array. Confidence: high for the two city parks and Milpitas Sports Center (city .gov + Pickleheads + multiple independent aggregators all agree on existence and court counts); high for Silicon Valley Table Tennis and Pickleball once the duplicate-listing question is accounted for; moderate for The Picklr given it opened literally one day before this research and has one unresolved indoor/outdoor discrepancy on its own website.

venues.name: Hall Memorial Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court (asphalt); 6 pickleball lines striped onto 2 of the park's 4 tennis courts under a city pilot program — one court split into 4 shared-use lines (PB1–PB4, pickleball has priority but tennis can still use it) and one court striped into 2 pickleball-exclusive lines (PB5–PB6); the other 2 tennis courts remain tennis-priority only
venues.netType: Nets provided by the City for the pilot program; most aggregators (including Pickleheads) describe them as permanent/dedicated, but the city's own program page only says nets are 'provided by the City' without specifying permanent vs. portable
venues.hours: 8am–9pm daily per aggregators; the city's own pilot-program page does not publish specific hours
venues.weather: South Bay warmth near the salt flats — sunnier and drier than the Peninsula, hot summer afternoons, mild winters with little fog.
venues.notes: IMPORTANT STATUS CAVEAT: these are not permanent dedicated pickleball courts. The City ran a one-year 'Pickle Ball Pilot Program' (Aug 2022–Aug 2023) that striped 2 of Hall Memorial Park's 4 tennis courts for pickleball; the striping remains in place and courts are actively playable today per 2026-dated sources (Pickleheads, Yelp updated June 2026), but converting the pilot to *permanent* status is still politically unresolved as of the most recent reporting found — Milpitas Pickleball Club's own advocacy page states a May 20, 2025 city council meeting 'passed a resolution allowing action on all proposed items, except for Hall Memorial Pickleball, leaving its future undecided.' Tennis players have organized opposition (per Milpitas Beat, Oct 2023: roughly '200+ regular tennis users' vs. concentrated pickleball demand). No source found dated after June 2025 confirms a final decision — a human should check for a 2025/2026 city council resolution before treating this as settled either way. Address confirmed as 304 La Honda Dr via the city's own facility page; one AI-synthesized search result said '374 La Honda Dr', contradicted by every other source checked (including Waze and Google Maps) — treated as that one source's transcription error. lat/lon sourced from mypacer.com's listing for this exact address; not independently cross-verified against a second precise geocode, so treat as good-not-perfect.

venues.name: Peter Gill Memorial Park
venues.surface: Outdoor asphalt; 2 pickleball courts re-striped onto the park's former handball courts under the same city pilot program as Hall Memorial Park, with an extended court area and new fencing added; pickleball has priority on these courts at all times per the city (unlike Hall Memorial Park's split tennis/pickleball arrangement)
venues.netType: Nets provided by the City; permanent vs. portable not specified by the official source
venues.hours: Park hours 6am–10pm daily per aggregators; the city's own pilot-program page does not publish specific court hours
venues.weather: South Bay warmth near the salt flats — sunnier and drier than the Peninsula, hot summer afternoons, mild winters with little fog.
venues.notes: Pickleheads lists this venue as 'Gill Park'; the city's own facility page calls it 'Gill Memorial Park' and most third-party listings (Yelp, Tripadvisor, DailyPB) call it 'Peter Gill Memorial Park' or 'Peter D Gill Memorial Park' — used the fuller, more common name here since the city page is this research's primary source, all clearly the same place. Same pilot-program caveat as Hall Memorial Park applies (started as a temporary one-year pilot, Aug 2022–Aug 2023, striping still in place and active today per 2026-dated sources), but Gill's courts were converted from underused handball courts rather than contested tennis courts, and no organized opposition to Gill's conversion turned up in any source checked — its situation reads as materially less contentious than Hall's. Could not confirm a precise geocoded lat/lon for 611 Paseo Refugio despite several attempts: one aggregator, MyPacer, lists a 'Peter T Gill Park' at a different address (669 Aberdeen Ct) and different acreage (15.7 vs. this park's official 8.1), which reads as either a data error on their part or a genuinely different location — that coordinate was deliberately not used here rather than risk pinning the wrong place. Could not confirm a numeric Google Maps cid either (only found a place_id, which per this file's rules doesn't qualify) — a human with map access should geocode this one directly.

venues.name: Milpitas Sports Center
venues.surface: Indoor wood court, permanent lines, portable nets
venues.netType: Portable nets on permanently-lined indoor courts
venues.price: $11 drop-in ($8 fee + $3 processing fee) or with a current Milpitas Sports Center membership, per the city's official Drop-In Sports page; other sources describe a separate $49/year sports-center membership plus a $10 (age 50+) or $20 (under 50) 5-visit pass — pricing may differ by program or have changed since those secondary sources were captured, call ahead to confirm
venues.hours: Offered as part of the Sports Center's multi-sport Drop-In Sports/Open Gym program, first-come first-served with no guaranteed spot (participants must be high-school age or older). Exact weekly pickleball schedule is reported inconsistently across sources and the city's own official page does not publish specific days/times: one aggregator says Tue 5–9pm & Fri 10am–1pm; another says Tue 5:30–8:45pm, Wed 8–11:30am, Fri 8–11:30am; an older (2023-era) source says Tue 5:30–9pm, Wed 8–11am, every-other-Fri 8–11am. Call 408-586-3225 or check the official page for the current schedule.
venues.notes: Address discrepancy: the city's own Drop-In Sports/Open Gym page lists the Sports Center's address as '457 E Calaveras Blvd', but every other independent source checked (Google Maps' own listing, Yelp, Waze, the city's separate Sports Center facility page) consistently gives 1325 E Calaveras Blvd — used 1325 here as the physical building address; 457 E Calaveras Blvd is almost certainly the Recreation & Community Services administrative office address (the Pickle Ball Pilot Program page separately lists that same 457 address as its own department contact), not this building. googleMapsUrl's cid was derived by converting the hex feature id in Google's own maps.google.com place URL for this address (0x7fa0dacca53afd90 → decimal) — same derivation method used in prior research files in this repo (see fremont.json). Courts here are temporary/removable pickleball markings on general-purpose gym courts shared with basketball/volleyball/badminton, not dedicated pickleball construction.

venues.name: Silicon Valley Table Tennis and Pickleball
venues.surface: Indoor acrylic, dedicated court with permanent net and lines
venues.price: $60 for 2 hours (2-hour minimum booking), up to 8 players
venues.notes: LIKELY DUPLICATE FOUND AND MERGED: Pickleheads separately lists a fifth Milpitas venue called 'Indoor Private Pickleball court' (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/milpitas/indoor-private-pickleball-court) that is almost certainly this exact same physical court, not a distinct sixth court somewhere else. Evidence: Bounce.game's scrape of that Pickleheads listing gives its address as 1827 Houret Ct — identical to this venue; both are described identically (1 indoor acrylic court, dedicated, permanent net and lines, private, reservable, restrooms/trainers-lessons/wheelchair-accessible/lights); and a Yelp review of 'Indoor Private Pickleball' quotes a price and format ('$60 for 2 hours... bring up to 8 people') that matches this venue's own pricing exactly, plus describes a hidden, unsigned entrance with a keypad code sent by text — consistent with this being a low-visibility court inside a business park unit rather than a genuinely separate marketed venue. Treated as one venue rather than two to avoid inflating Milpitas's court count; Pickleheads' stated citywide total of '5 locations, 16 courts' should probably be read as 4 real locations / 15 real courts once this duplicate is collapsed (see top-level notes). The other Pickleheads listing's $50/hour figure is preserved here only as a secondary data point since it conflicts with the $60-for-2-hours rate on the club's own site (used as authoritative here since it's the primary source for this venue). Also found and deliberately excluded: a now-closed 'Silicon Valley Table Tennis Club' location at 718 Montague Expy, Milpitas (Yelp: 'CLOSED - Updated November 2024') — an old address for the same club, not a second active venue. Could not confirm a precise geocode or a numeric Google Maps cid for 1827 Houret Ct despite several attempts.

venues.name: The Picklr - Milpitas
venues.surface: PPA-quality court surfacing per an independent court-finder aggregator (CourtSource); the facility's own website separately lists 'Outdoor Surfacing' as an amenity bullet, which conflicts with that same page's own <title> tag ('Indoor Pickleball Courts in Milpitas, CA'), a contemporaneous Milpitas Beat news article ('premium indoor pickleball club'), and The Picklr's uniformly-indoor national brand — see notes, this is unresolved
venues.price: $199/month unlimited membership (after a $1 pre-purchase deposit) or $1,990/year unlimited; Founder membership tiers (2 and 3) sold out before opening
amenities: Locker rooms and showers (TBD per facility's own site)
venues.notes: NOT on Pickleheads as of this research date — found instead via general web search, the club's own website, and a Milpitas Beat news article. Included anyway per this research's brief (every real public/membership venue confirmable to exist, not only Pickleheads-listed ones). Brand new: the facility's own site states 'Doors Open: July 10, 2026', i.e. the day before this research was conducted, so some operational details may not be fully settled (the facility's own site marks locker rooms/showers as 'TBD'). An October 2025 Milpitas Beat article had projected a 'May 2026' opening; treated as a stale projection superseded by the venue's own live site's July 10, 2026 date, since construction-timeline slippage is common and the live site is the more current source. UNRESOLVED CONFLICT, why confirmed is false here: the facility's own website lists 'Outdoor Surfacing' under amenities with no other indoor/outdoor language on that page, which contradicts that identical page's own title tag ('Indoor Pickleball Courts in Milpitas, CA'), an independent aggregator (CourtSource: 'climate-controlled indoor facility... 16 total courts, all indoors'), and a local news feature (Milpitas Beat, Oct 2025: 'premium indoor pickleball club') — and runs against The Picklr's national brand identity, which is uniformly indoor/climate-controlled at every other location spot-checked. Set indoorOutdoor to 'Indoor' as the best-evidence call (multiple independent signals vs. one self-sourced anomalous amenity bullet) but left confirmed:false and documented the conflict here rather than quietly resolving it — a human who can visit in person or call/email (milpitas.ca@thepicklrfranchise.com) should verify directly. Could not confirm a precise geocode or numeric Google Maps cid for 128 Ranch Dr / McCarthy Ranch this soon after opening.
